members.name: of the
members.details: Community Nutrition Core Group – IAPEN India, contributing to the strengthening of community-focused nutrition initiatives at the national level. She previously served as the Secretary of the IAPEN India Lucknow Chapter, where she supported academic programming, professional engagement, and community outreach. She also serves as a Faculty for the Certified Diabetes Educator (CDE) Program under the Nutrition & Diabetes India Core Group of IAPEN India. Beyond IAPEN India, Ranu leads the Community Nutrition and Public Health Committee of the PDA and has earlier served as the State Head (Uttar Pradesh) for the Global Nutrition Partnership Program of IAPEN India–ASPEN, contributing to interdisciplinary collaboration and capacity building. Ranu is frequently invited as a guest faculty at universities, colleges, and medical universities, delivering academic and capacity-building sessions for postgraduate students of Community Medicine, paramedical and allied health sciences students, and health professionals. She is actively involved in training government functionaries, including ASHA workers, Anganwadi workers, District Programme Officers (DPOs), and Child Development Project Officers (CDPOs), and has organized Anganwadi-level training programs to support effective grassroots implementation of nutrition and public health interventions. A committed community nutrition practitioner, Ranu works extensively with women of reproductive age in urban slums and rural communities, focusing on balanced diets, holistic lifestyle practices, sanitation, and hygiene to improve nutritional knowledge, health behaviours, and overall health outcomes. She is also deeply engaged in school and college-based nutrition education, promoting healthy eating habits, menstrual hygiene, and WASH (Water, Sanitation, and Hygiene) practices. As part of her school health initiatives, Ranu conducts regular nutrition education sessions and health awareness camps in government and private schools, with a strong emphasis on rural areas and primary schools. Her interventions are age-appropriate, interactive, and practical, ensuring key messages are easily understood. In primary schools, she emphasizes healthy eating habits, basic hygiene, balanced diets, and anemia prevention through simple demonstrations and participatory activities. For older children and adolescents, her sessions address nutrition during growth, micronutrient deficiencies, physical activity, and the role of WASH in disease prevention. These efforts aim to promote sustained behaviour change, improved nutritional awareness, and better health outcomes, particularly in underserved rural communities. Her community and school-based interventions focus on the prevention and management of anemia, diabetes, hypertension, obesity, and other lifestyle-related diseases through behaviour change communication, practical dietary modifications, and culturally appropriate community-based education. Key areas of work include interactive nutrition education, community-led WASH demonstrations, adolescent reproductive health and nutrition education, nutrition counselling for pregnant and lactating women, and the promotion of affordable, home-based dietary improvement strategies. Academically, Ranu is a PhD Scholar with an MSc in Food and Nutrition. She is a Certified Diabetes Educator (IDF)and has completed specialized training in FODMAPs from Monash University, Australia. Her qualifications include multiple public health–focused postgraduate programs from the Public Health Foundation of India (PHFI) in Public Health and Nutrition and Management of Reproductive and Child Health. She also holds a Fellowship in Clinical Nutrition, is a Certified Maternal and Child Nutritionist, and has earned a Master Certificate in Programming for Infant and Young Child Feeding from UNICEF and Cornell University. Additionally, she has completed the MIYCAN program and specialized training in WASH, Humanitarian WASH Coordination, and Early Childhood Programming from UNICEF. Further strengthening her expertise, Ranu has completed over 17 certificate courses from the European Society for Clinical Nutrition and Metabolism (ESPEN), reflecting her strong commitment to continuous professional development and evidence-based practice. She is a co-author of three books, including Diabetes Mellitus: Nutrition, Functional Foods & Nutraceuticals, Sustainable Nutrition: A Guide for Dietitians to Health and Planetary Well-being, and Media’s Grip: Unravelling Media’s Influence on Society. She has authored over 150 articles in leading newspapers and magazines and has presented research papers at international platforms, including ESPEN Congresses in Lyon, Milan, and Prague. In recognition of her sustained contributions to public health nutrition, Ranu has been felicitated by the Hon’ble Governor of Uttar Pradesh, Smt. Anandiben Patel, at Raj Bhavan. Her professional honours include the National Quality Award for Excellence in Public Health Nutrition (ICNC 2023, Chennai) and the State Level Quality Award (ICNC 2025, Varanasi), both conferred by IAPEN India. Her professional affiliations include Life Membership in IAPEN India, the Indian Dietetic Association (IDA), Society for Renal Nutrition and Metabolism (SRNM), Nutrition Society of India (NSI), Indian Public Health Association (IPHA), and the Poshan Dhara Association (PDA). She is also an Associate Member of the American Diabetes Association, American Society of Nutrition, and International Society of Hypertension, and a member of the South Asian Association of Parenteral and Enteral Nutrition and ESPEN.

members.name: Dr. Suneeta S. Chandorkar PhD
members.details: Associate Professor (Grade 3), Foods & Nutrition The Maharaja Sayajirao University of Baroda, India Education and Experience Dr. Suneeta S. Chandorkar is an Associate Professor in the Department of Foods & Nutrition at The Maharaja Sayajirao University of Baroda, India, with over 27 years of experience in teaching, research, and policy engagement across Food Science and Public Health Nutrition. She has guided and mentored numerous postgraduate and doctoral scholars, contributing significantly to academic capacity building. Awards Recipient of multiple international and national awards for excellence in chrono nutrition, dietary sustainability, food labelling, metabolic health, and GIS-based nutrition research, including Young Investigator, First Prize/Excellent Abstract awards (IUNS, Apollo ICNU,IPHACON, ANH Academy) and the Young Scientist Award (NSI 2024). Recognised for research leadership and mentorship, including Mapathon Champion (IIT Bombay–FOSSEE), and recipient of the C. Gopalan-Srikantia Gold Medal for academic excellence (M.Sc., MSU Baroda). Research Dr. Chandorkar’s research spans food labelling and nutrient profiling, food safety, sustainable food systems, GIS-based nutrition mapping, chrononutrition, DOHaD, and sports nutrition. Her interdisciplinary work integrates laboratory analysis, population surveys, and GIS tools to inform national and global nutrition policy, including contributions to FSSAI, CODEX, WHO, and G20 (Brazil 2024). Extension Activities Dr. Chandorkar’s extension work focuses on capacity building, community nutrition interventions, and knowledge translation, working closely with government agencies, NGOs, academic institutions, and industry. She leads training programmes, policy dialogues, and technology transfer initiatives that translate research evidence into actionable nutrition, food safety, and sustainable food system practices across diverse populations.

members.name: Dr. Subramania Iyer Professor
members.details: and Chairman MS (Otorhinolaryngology), MCh (Plastic Surgery), FRCS Speciality: Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery, Advanced Centre for Robotic Surgery, Head and Neck Surgery and Oncology, Surgical Oncology, Composite Tissue Allotransplantation With over four decades of unparalleled expertise, Dr. Subramania Iyer stands as a titan in the realm of surgical innovation and excellence. Having meticulously performed over 40,000+ major surgical procedures and more than 6,000+ free flap surgeries, his mastery in the field is undisputed. Dr. Iyer's accolades include pioneering the first-hand transplants in the country and leading the centre with largest number of composite tissue allotransplants globally, a testament to his groundbreaking contributions. Dr. Iyer's special interests lie in head and neck cancer surgery, secondary reconstruction, laryngeal reinnervation, and cutting-edge techniques like laser and robotic surgery, where he continues to push the boundaries of medical advancement. His contributions in field of medical training are pathbreaking with establishing the first three-year structured training programme in head and neck surgical oncology and Indias first and only masters training programme in deglutology and swallowing therapy.

members.name: Dr. Krishnakumar Thankappan Clinical Professor
members.details: MS (ENT), DNB (Otorhinolarygology), MCh (Head and Neck Surgical Oncology) Fellow, American Head and Neck Society Speciality: Advanced Centre for Robotic Surgery, Head and Neck Surgery and Oncology, Surgical Oncology Dr. Krishnakumar currently serves as Professor at the Department of Head and Surgery and Oncology at Amrita Institute of Medical Sciences (Amrita Hospital). He did his MBBS at T. D. Medical College, Alappuzha and obtained MS (ENT) from B.J. Medical College Pune in January 2002. During May 2002 - August 2005, he worked as a consultant ENT surgeon at KVM Hospital, Cherthala. Later, he joined Amrita Institute of Medical Sciences to obtain his MCh (Head and Neck Surgical Oncology) and successfully completed the same in June 2008. In July 2008, he joined Roswell Park Cancer Institute, Buffalo, New York for a two-year fellowship program in Head and Neck Surgical Oncology. During the first year of the fellowship program, Dr. Krishnakumar was actively involved in research projects on the development of newer photosensitizers for photodynamic therapy (PDT) for head and neck tumors. During the second year, he was exposed to the entire clinical aspects covering ablative and reconstructive head and neck surgery with exposure to endoscopic skull base surgery. After completing the fellowship program, he joined Amrita Hospital in August 2010 as Associate Professor.Having over 17 years of clinical experience and 10 years of academic experience, he has presented and published papers in national and international forums. Won the Young Investigator Award, at the Indian Co-operative Oncology network. He has more than 40 publications in international journals. He is the Regional Editor (Asia Pacific) of the Journal of Craniomaxillofacial Trauma and Reconstruction (AOCMF) and is a journal reviewer of more than 10 international journals including The Laryngoscope, Clinical Otolaryngology, Head and Neck. Has written a book titled “Basic Concepts in Head and Neck Surgery and Oncology”. World swallowing week is observed Dec 12th-18th to raise awareness about Dysphagia- difficulty in swallowing. This hidden disability is seen in many disease conditions like post stroke, parkinsons, head and neck cancer and also in seniors and post Covid. This video which is an initiative by Dysphagia Core Group-IAPEN India is to raise awareness about this swallowing difficulty among the public. 50 dietitians across the country have recorded this video in 16 Indian Languages so this important message reaches the masses. The message is ” Screen for Dysphagia- PREVENT Malnutrition “.

members.details: Nasreen is a nutrition academician and researcher with over 15 years of experience in teaching, clinical nutrition, and research. members.details: FNASc, FRCP (Edin), FIAN, FNAMS Professor & Head Department of Neurology AIIMS, New Educational Qualifications: MBBS: MKCG Medical College, Brahmapur, ODISHA, 1991 DM: NIMHANS, Bengaluru, KARNATAKA (Neurology, 1996) Research Specialization : (Major scientific fields of interest): Dr Manjari Tripathi is a Professor of Neurology at AIIMS, New Delhi, with specialization in epilepsy. She has over 20 years of experience in managing drug-refractory epilepsy and runs the epilepsy monitoring unit at AIIMS, New Delhi. Besides clinical research, Dr Tripathi has been engaged in cutting edge basic epilepsy research, particularly those involving next generation sequencing-based methodologies, to understand the mechanisms of drug resistance and epileptogenesis in refractory epilepsies. Dr Tripathi's work is focused on epilepsy mechanisms, therapies, care and management, and has authored and co-authored over 500 articles in national and international journals of repute on epilepsy and related areas. As a part of collaborative effort Dr Tripathi is the Co-Principal Investigator of the Centre of Excellence for Epilepsy which grants her access to wet-lab with specialization in “multi-omics” approach for discovery of epileptogenic and drug resistance mechanisms in epilepsy. These include, whole-genome sequencing, mRNA/miRNA sequencing, and mass spectrometry-based lipidomics and proteomics. Awards/Honors : - 1st Prize for AIIMS Excellence Research Award for the year 2017-2018, AIIMS, New Delhi. - Faculty branding awards 2013 Certificate of Excellence by EET CRS Research wing for Excellence in Professional Education and Industry. - Awarded Anandi Bai Joshi award for Medicine in Third IRDS awards 2012. - Awarded- DBT Young Investigator award- for fMRI and EEG as an essential tool in the Noninvasive investigation of patients with epilepsy which is Intractable. - Gold medallist in MBBS: Anatomy, Physiology, Medicine.
